input.background: Refer to the advertising-monthly-sales data presented in Table 11.1. Consider the straight-line model, $E(y)=\beta_0+\beta_1 x$, where $y=$ sales revenue (thousands of dollars) and $x=$ advertising expenditure (hundreds of dollars).
input.question: Predict the sales revenue when advertising expenditure is $\$ 200$ (i.e., when $x=2$).

output.answer:
The predicted value of $y$ for a given value of $x$ can be obtained by substituting into the formula for the least squares line. Substituting $x=2$ into the least squares equation yields

$$
\hat{y}=-.1+.7 x=-.1+.7(2)=1.3
$$

Thus, when advertising expenditure is $\$ 200$, we predict monthly sales revenue to be $\$ 1,300$. We show how to find a prediction interval for $y$ in Section 11.6.
